question.text: Under which section of the SEBI Act does SEBI have the same powers as a civil court under the Code of Civil Procedure 1908?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(3) of the SEBI Act provides SEBI with the same powers as are vested in a civil court under CPC 1908, in respect of matters such as inspection of books and registers, summoning and enforcing attendance of persons, and examining them on oath. **The Trap:** Section 11(2) lists regulatory powers, Section 11(4) lists interim protective measures, and Section 11B covers directions. The civil court equivalence is specifically under 11(3). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision gives SEBI quasi-judicial powers for investigation and enforcement — critical for ensuring compliance with securities laws.
